title: Cache Performance, Scope, Integrity, and Poisoning Defense · Mastery Check
summary: A release board compares near-miss decisions for cache performance, scope, integrity, and poisoning defense and requires the learner to defend the smallest safe cache threat and measurement policy. Planned artifact: Record independent decisions and explanations for cache threat and measurement policy. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Separate disposable performance caches from immutable artifacts, job outputs, external registries, and release records by trust and lifetime.
Construct keys from OS, architecture, tool, dependency lock, relevant configuration, and cache version with bounded restore fallback.
Restrict who can save or restore executable caches across fork, branch, event, workflow, and privilege boundaries.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# cicd-github-actions cumulative delivery-system portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic workflow, policy, artifact, and release evidence review only.
# They never call GitHub APIs, start runners, execute learner commands, access Docker or registries, exchange OIDC tokens, or contact cloud services.
workspace: cicd
version-baseline:
  checkout: 7.0.0
  setup-node: 7.0.0
  upload-artifact: 7.0.1
  download-artifact: 8.0.1
  attest: 4.1.1
  node: 24-lts
  typescript-native: 7.0.2
  typescript-api-compatibility: 6.0.2
  build-push-action: 7
  google-auth: 3
  deploy-cloudrun: 3
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-delivery-environment
evidence:

steps.title: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence
steps.instruction:
quiz case — A quiz privileged release restores executable cache content written by a fork through a broad fallback key. Compare cold and warm time, key scope, save authority, poisoning risk, and immutable artifact separation. Competency probe: Measure cold and warm dependency, build, test, upload, queue, and critical-path time while preserving a regular cold-path regression run.

The visible result looks correct, but its invariant or value contract is unverified. What must happen next?

Evidence target: Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence.
steps.why: The decision supplies observable evidence for measure cold and warm dependency, build, test, upload, queue, and critical-path time while preserving a regular cold-path regression run. For Diagnose the hidden failure · Quiz Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence, keep Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.text: A quiz privileged release restores executable cache content written by a fork through a broad fallback key. Compare cold and warm time, key scope, save authority, poisoning risk, and immutable artifact separation. Competency probe: Measure cold and warm dependency, build, test, upload, queue, and critical-path time while preserving a regular cold-path regression run.

content.text: Select evidence that identifies a cause, not a patch that only hides the Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence symptom.

Compare cold and warm time, key scope, save authority, poisoning risk, and immutable artifact separation. Competency probe: Measure cold and warm dependency, build, test, upload, queue, and critical-path time while preserving a regular cold-path regression run.

stimulus.title: Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence failure evidence
stimulus.caption: A plausible visible result conflicts with the governing contract and requires a cause-level repair.

lines.label: observed
lines.text: A warm-cache improvement proves the complete pipeline is faster.

lines.label: required
lines.text: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Measure cold and warm dependency, build, test, upload, queue, and critical-path time while preserving a regular cold-path regression run.

options.text: For diagnose the hidden failure, copy one familiar workflow, omit event and revision identity, permissions, runner, immutable artifact, failure, rollback, and disposable delivery-environment transfer checks, then accept a green run as production proof.
Run the boundary case, inspect the actual mechanism, then accept or repair the claim.
Accept the result because visible output is the highest authority. That claim does not survive the diagnose the hidden failure evidence boundary.

steps.title: Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence
steps.instruction:
Write the quiz evidence note for Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A quiz privileged release restores executable cache content written by a fork through a broad fallback key. Compare cold and warm time, key scope, save authority, poisoning risk, and immutable artifact separation. Competency probe: Measure cold and warm dependency, build, test, upload, queue, and critical-path time while preserving a regular cold-path regression run.

For Defend one difficult decision · Quiz Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence, keep Ci Cold Warm Performance Evidence connected to mechanically reviewable workflow, trust, artifact, deployment, and recovery evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Measure cold and warm dependency, build, test, upload, queue, and critical-path time while preserving a regular cold-path regression run.
